events.title: Fuel oil spill from truck fitting leak (~100 liters) in Inuvik, Northwest Territories
events.description: A truck fitting leak released about 100 liters of petroleum fuel oil onto land in Inuvik in the Beaufort Delta region. The spill involved fuels described as Jet A/diesel/turbo A/heating fuel.

location.name: Inuvik, Northwest Territories, Canada (spill site: Bonnet Plume Rd/Centennial St/Inuit Rd)

sources.source_name: Government of Northwest Territories (ECC) Spill Database
sources.headline: Fuel oil spill from truck due to fitting leak (100 liters) in Inuvik, Northwest Territories, Canada

key_quotes.quote: released approximately 100 liters of petroleum fuel oil

dedupe.cluster_hint: GNWT spill database record 1995125

events.title: Gasoline spill from tank farm overflow (~100 liters) in Repulse Bay (Naujaat), Nunavut
events.description: An overflow incident at the Petroleum Products Division tank farm in Repulse Bay reportedly released about 100 liters of petroleum gasoline. The fuels are described as aviation/turbo B/jet B.

location.name: Repulse Bay (Naujaat), Nunavut, Canada (Petroleum Products Div. Tank Farm)

sources.source_name: Government of Northwest Territories (ECC) Spill Database (advanced search)
sources.headline: Gasoline spill at Petroleum Products Division tank farm due to overflow event (100 liters) in Repulse Bay, Nunavut, Canada

key_quotes.quote: overflow event ... released approximately 100 liters of petroleum gasoline

events.title: Hurricane Erin landfalls in Florida (Vero Beach area) and the Florida Panhandle
events.description: Hurricane Erin made landfall in Florida on August 2, 1995 and again in the Florida Panhandle on August 3, 1995. The storm brought hurricane conditions and heavy rainfall to affected areas.

sources.headline: Hurricane Erin
1995 Tropical Cyclone Reports (index)

key_quotes.quote: Hurricane Erin made landfall in Florida on August 2, 1995 and again on the Florida Panhandle on August 3, 1995.

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1995 Atlantic hurricane Erin landfalls Florida

events.title: Hurricane Felix (1995) persists as a major hurricane over the western Atlantic
events.description: Hurricane Felix was active from August 8–22, 1995 as a major hurricane over the western Atlantic. The storm produced hazardous marine conditions and contributed to coastal impacts such as high surf affecting nearby regions including the U.S. East Coast and Bermuda area.

sources.headline: Tropical Cyclone Report: Hurricane Felix
1995 Tropical Cyclone Reports (index)

key_quotes.quote: Hurricane Felix was a major Atlantic hurricane active from August 8–22, 1995.

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1995 Atlantic hurricane Felix western Atlantic/Bermuda

events.title: Tropical Storm Gabrielle makes landfall in Tamaulipas, Mexico bringing heavy rainfall
events.description: Tropical Storm Gabrielle developed in the Gulf of Mexico and made landfall in northeastern Mexico on August 11, 1995. Heavy rainfall associated with the storm produced flooding impacts in Mexico and parts of southern Texas.

sources.headline: Gabrielle (1995) rainfall summary
1995 Tropical Cyclone Reports (index)

key_quotes.quote: Tropical Storm Gabrielle developed in the Gulf of Mexico and made landfall in northeastern Mexico on August 11, 1995.

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1995 Atlantic TS Gabrielle landfall Tamaulipas

events.title: Hurricane Flossie brings heavy rain impacts to Baja California Sur and contributes to flooding in Arizona
events.description: Hurricane Flossie was active from August 7–14, 1995. Heavy rainfall affected parts of Mexico’s Baja California Peninsula, and moisture associated with the system contributed to flash flooding impacts in Arizona.

sources.headline: 1995 Eastern North Pacific Tropical Cyclone Reports (index)

key_quotes.quote: Hurricane Flossie was active August 7–14, 1995, bringing heavy rainfall to parts of Baja California Sur.

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1995 EPAC hurricane Flossie impacts Baja California Sur/Arizona

events.title: Tropical Storm Jerry brings heavy rainfall and flooding across parts of the southeastern United States
events.description: Tropical Storm Jerry affected the southeastern United States during August 22–28, 1995. The storm produced heavy rainfall and flooding across parts of Florida, Georgia, and the Carolinas.

sources.headline: Tropical Cyclone Report: Tropical Storm Jerry
1995 Tropical Cyclone Reports (index)

key_quotes.quote: Tropical Storm Jerry affected the southeastern U.S. during August 22–28, 1995, producing heavy rainfall and flooding.

events.title: Mw 7.7 earthquake in the Bougainville region, Papua New Guinea (Aug 16, 1995)
events.description: An Mw 7.7 earthquake was reported in the Bougainville region of Papua New Guinea on August 16, 1995 (UTC).

events.title: Penticton interface wildfire reported contained (Ellis Creek Canyon/Carmi subdivision)
events.description: A British Columbia government backgrounder states the Penticton interface fire (Ellis Creek Canyon/Carmi subdivision) was contained on August 5. The same backgrounder notes evacuations and structural losses during the incident.

sources.source_name: Government of British Columbia News Archive

key_quotes.quote: the incident was contained on August 5 ... later controlled on August 18

dedupe.cluster_hint: Penticton interface fire (Ellis Creek Canyon/Carmi subdivision), 1995 backgrounder dates

events.title: Penticton interface wildfire reported controlled (Ellis Creek Canyon/Carmi subdivision)
events.description: A British Columbia government backgrounder states the Penticton interface fire (Ellis Creek Canyon/Carmi subdivision) was controlled on August 18, following earlier containment. The item frames this as part of a broader summary of the incident’s impacts.

sources.source_name: Government of British Columbia News Archive

events.title: Black Mountain wildfire threatened homes near Rancho Peñasquitos (San Diego)
events.description: A Los Angeles Times report describes a fast-moving wildfire on Black Mountain near Rancho Peñasquitos that burned over 100 acres and threatened homes. The report says it started in the afternoon and was contained later the same day.

location.name: Black Mountain (Rancho Peñasquitos area), San Diego, California, USA

sources.headline: Black Mountain wildfire threatened homes before being contained

key_quotes.quote: A fast-moving wildfire on Black Mountain charred more than 100 acres and threatened several dozen homes

dedupe.cluster_hint: Black Mountain fire near Rancho Peñasquitos, San Diego, Aug 1995

events.title: Coral bleaching and mortality reported during late-August 1995 warming on Costa Rica’s Caribbean coast
events.description: A late-August 1995 warming event under doldrum conditions in the Western Caribbean coincided with substantial coral reef bleaching and documented mortality at monitored sites in Cahuita and Gandoca-Manzanillo, Costa Rica. Bleaching was observed to depths of about 20 meters with notable impacts across several coral taxa.

location.name: Parque Nacional Cahuita and Refugio Nacional de Vida Silvestre Gandoca-Manzanillo, Limón Province, Costa Rica

sources.headline: Coral bleaching and mortality during late-August 1995 warming event on the Caribbean coast of Costa Rica (Cahuita and Gandoca-Manzanillo)

key_quotes.quote: ...late August 1995... warming event... coincided with substantial coral reef organism bleaching and documented mortality...

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1995-08 Caribbean Costa Rica coral bleaching Cahuita Gandoca-Manzanillo

events.title: USFWS press release recommends no critical habitat designation for Gulf sturgeon
events.description: A U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service press release dated Aug. 21, 1995 reported the agency’s recommendation not to designate critical habitat for the threatened Gulf sturgeon. The announcement reflected an official step in federal conservation policy affecting protections for the species.

sources.headline: U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service recommends no critical habitat be designated

key_quotes.quote: ...press release dated Aug 21, 1995... recommends no critical habitat be designated... Gulf sturgeon...

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1995-08 USFWS Gulf sturgeon critical habitat recommendation

events.title: USFWS proposes ESA listing for seven Southern California mountain plant taxa
events.description: On Aug. 2, 1995, the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service published a proposed rule to list seven plant taxa from Southern California mountains as endangered or threatened. The proposal cited restricted ranges and multiple threats, representing a federal regulatory action aimed at biodiversity protection.

sources.headline: Endangered and Threatened Wildlife and Plants; Proposed Endangered or Threatened Status for Seven Plants From the Mountains of Southern California

key_quotes.quote: ...Proposed Endangered or Threatened Status for Seven Plants From the Mountains of Southern California...

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1995-08 Federal Register proposed listing seven plants Southern California mountains

events.title: USFWS proposes threatened status for pallid manzanita (Arctostaphylos pallida)
events.description: On Aug. 2, 1995, the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service published a proposed rule to list pallid manzanita (Arctostaphylos pallida) as threatened. The proposal described risks including habitat impacts and small, isolated populations, as part of an ESA regulatory process.

sources.headline: Endangered and Threatened Wildlife and Plants; Proposed Threatened Status for Arctostaphylos pallida (Pallid Manzanita)

key_quotes.quote: ...Proposed Threatened Status for Arctostaphylos pallida (Pallid Manzanita)...

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1995-08 Federal Register proposed threatened status Arctostaphylos pallida

events.title: EPA final rule amending Transportation Conformity rule (transition to control strategy period)
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency published a final rule on transportation conformity, aligning certain consequences of state air quality planning failures with the timing of Clean Air Act highway sanctions. The final rule was published in the Federal Register on August 7, 1995, with an effective date of August 8, 1995.

sources.headline: Transportation Conformity Rule Amendments—Transition to the Control Strategy Period
Transportation conformity: chronological list of rulemakings

key_quotes.quote: Transportation Conformity Rule Amendments—Transition to the Control Strategy Period

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA Transportation Conformity Rule Amendments (1995-08)

events.title: EPA final rule amending refrigerant leak repair provisions under Clean Air Act section 608
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency published a final rule under the Protection of Stratospheric Ozone program, amending refrigerant recycling regulations and revising leak repair provisions under Clean Air Act section 608. The rule was published in the Federal Register on August 8, 1995.

sources.headline: Protection of Stratospheric Ozone—Supplemental rule amending leak repair provisions under Clean Air Act section 608

key_quotes.quote: Protection of Stratospheric Ozone

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA Section 608 leak repair provisions (1995-08)

events.title: Executive Order 12969 on federal acquisition and Community Right-to-Know reporting
events.description: President Bill Clinton signed Executive Order 12969 concerning federal acquisition and Community Right-to-Know, directing procurement policy toward contracting (to the maximum extent practicable) with companies that publicly report toxic chemical releases. The order was signed on August 8, 1995.

sources.headline: Executive Order 12969
U.S. Code (reference page)

key_quotes.quote: Executive Order 12969

dedupe.cluster_hint: Executive Order 12969 (1995-08-08)

events.title: EPA final NESHAP for petroleum refineries
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency published a final National Emission Standards for Hazardous Air Pollutants (NESHAP) rule for petroleum refineries, establishing controls for hazardous air pollutant emissions from refinery process vents, storage vessels, equipment leaks, and wastewater streams. The final rule was published in the Federal Register on August 18, 1995.

sources.headline: National Emission Standards for Hazardous Air Pollutants—Petroleum Refineries

key_quotes.quote: National Emission Standards for Hazardous Air Pollutants—Petroleum Refineries

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA NESHAP Petroleum Refineries (1995-08-18)

events.title: U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service final rule approving export of American alligators taken in 1995–1997
events.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service published a final rule approving export of legally taken American alligators and products from approved states for the 1995–1997 harvest seasons under CITES. The rule addressed monitoring and tagging/export conditions and was published in the Federal Register on August 21, 1995.

sources.headline: Export approval for American alligators taken in 1995–1997 (CITES Appendix II)
